acceptanceCriteria: A clean-session smoke flow reaches preflight, contract, assessment, route, and attempt-start without a manually authored routing-input JSON, and the smoke run is documented as evidence.
A SPIKE-ROUTE-1-like low-complexity fixture assessment deterministically selects an eligible economy candidate through rigor route.
An ambiguous or low-confidence fixture stops with requires-review or escalates instead of silently selecting an economy candidate.

The /rigor:assess skill reports task characteristics (complexity, ambiguity, novelty, verification strength) with repository-path-anchored evidence and explicit confidence, and never names or selects a model; candidate selection remains a rigor route CLI decision.

The /rigor:assess skill runs deterministic validation (rigor route --dry-run with the generated rigor.routing-input.v2) before any routing plan is recorded, and documents the stop paths for low confidence, contradictory evidence, missing acceptance criteria, and required human decision.

The /rigor:orchestrate skill accepts an autonomous flow in which the routing input comes from /rigor:assess instead of a human-authored JSON argument, while still permitting an explicit human-provided routing input.
Skill and orchestration tests cover both the allowed (economy-selected) flow and the stopped (requires-review) flow using checked-in fixtures that exercise the built CLI.
Configured runtime identity remains recorded as unverified everywhere; nothing presents the assessment or the selection as runtime attestation.
node scripts/validate-plugin.mjs and claude plugin validate . --strict pass with the new skill registered.
constraints: Implement only the Scope and Acceptance criteria of issue #11; do not add unrelated features.
Do not modify src/**, dist/**, schemas/**, bin/**, hooks/**, agents/**, .rigor/**, or .github/**; if a runtime, schema, or CLI change appears necessary, stop instead of expanding scope.
Do not delete, weaken, or skip existing tests; new tests may be added.
Keep policy enforcement in the deterministic CLI; the skill prose must not move enforcement into prompts.
Treat Claude's assessment as reviewable judgment, never as a deterministic fact; validation authority stays with rigor route.
The assess skill must instruct stopping for low confidence, contradictory evidence, missing acceptance criteria, or a required human decision.
This implementation must be driven by the existing manually-invoked /rigor:orchestrate flow; the new assess flow must not bootstrap its own unreviewed contract during this task.
